plan_metadata.title: PHCA-DP-SGD: Post-Hoc Compression-Aware DP Training Experiment Plan
plan_metadata.gpu_allocation: Single GPU (all experiments run sequentially due to DP-SGD memory requirements)

experiments.title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
experiments.description: Install required packages for differential privacy, deep learning, and compression experiments

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch with CUDA: pip install torch==2.1.0 torchvision==0.16.0 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118
steps.step3: Install Opacus for DP-SGD: pip install opacus==1.4.0
steps.step4: Install additional dependencies: pip install numpy scipy matplotlib seaborn pandas scikit-learn tqdm tensorboard
steps.step5: Install compression libraries: pip install torch-pruning
steps.step6: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step7: Create directory structure: mkdir -p data results/{models,plots,tables} src/{models,data,utils,attacks}

experiments.title: Download and Prepare Datasets
experiments.description: Download CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and Purchase-100 datasets with proper splits for membership inference evaluation

steps.step4: Create train/test splits for CIFAR: Standard 50K/10K split, normalize with mean=[0.4914, 0.4822, 0.4465], std=[0.2470, 0.2435, 0.2616] for CIFAR-10
steps.step5: For membership inference attacks, further split training data: 45K for training, 5K as 'shadow' members (for MIA training)
steps.step6: Preprocess Purchase-100: 600 features, 100 classes, 197,324 samples. Split: 150K train, 47K test. Normalize features to [0,1]
steps.step7: Save dataset statistics to results/dataset_stats.json: sizes, class distributions, feature dimensions

experiments.title: Standard DP-SGD Implementation
experiments.description: Implement vanilla DP-SGD baseline using Opacus library as the foundation for comparison

steps.step1: Implement ResNet-18 for CIFAR (modified first conv: kernel=3, stride=1, padding=1; remove maxpool): src/models/resnet.py
steps.step2: Implement 4-layer ConvNet (128-256-512-512 channels, BN, ReLU, MaxPool, FC): src/models/convnet.py
steps.step3: Implement MLP for Purchase-100 (input=600, hidden=[1024,512,256], output=100): src/models/mlp.py
steps.step4: Create training script with Opacus: src/train_standard_dp.py
steps.step5: Hyperparameters for CIFAR-10: batch_size=256, epochs=30, lr=0.1 (decay 0.9 every 10 epochs), max_grad_norm=1.0
steps.step6: Privacy parameters: target_epsilon=[1, 3, 8], delta=1e-5, noise_multiplier computed via Opacus privacy engine
steps.step7: Run experiments on CIFAR-10 with ResNet-18 for 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) at epsilon=3

experiments.title: Standard DP-SGD + Post-Hoc Compression
experiments.description: Apply post-hoc compression to standard DP-SGD trained models to establish the current practice baseline

steps.step1: Implement magnitude pruning: src/compression/pruning.py with function apply_magnitude_pruning(model, sparsity_ratio)
steps.step2: Implement global magnitude pruning across all layers
steps.step3: Test compression ratios: sparsity=[0.5, 0.7, 0.9] (50%, 70%, 90% parameters pruned)
steps.step4: For each trained standard DP-SGD model, apply pruning and evaluate test accuracy
steps.step5: Record: pre-compression accuracy, post-compression accuracy, compression ratio, actual sparsity achieved

experiments.title: Adamczewski et al. (2023) Pre-Pruning Baseline
experiments.description: Implement pre-pruning approach that uses 'public' data for parameter selection before DP training

steps.step2: Simulate public data by using a held-out subset (10% of training data, not counted in privacy budget)
steps.step3: On 'public' data: Train non-private model for 10 epochs to identify important parameters
steps.step4: Apply magnitude pruning to obtain subnetwork with target sparsity (70%)
steps.step5: Reset remaining parameters and train only the subnetwork with DP-SGD
steps.step6: Gradient-dropping: During training, only compute gradients for retained parameters
steps.step7: Run for CIFAR-10, ResNet-18, epsilon=3, seeds=[42, 123, 456]
steps.step8: Record final accuracy and compare against standard DP-SGD + post-hoc compression

experiments.title: AdaDPIGU-style Binary Masking Baseline
experiments.description: Implement importance-based gradient selection with binary masking (simplified version of AdaDPIGU)

steps.step2: Pretraining phase (consumes privacy budget): Run 5 epochs of DP-SGD to accumulate per-parameter gradient magnitudes
steps.step3: Compute importance scores: exponential moving average of |gradient| for each parameter
steps.step4: Create binary mask: retain top 60% of parameters by importance (matching AdaDPIGU paper)
steps.step5: Main training: Apply binary mask - zero out gradients for masked parameters during updates
steps.step6: Use adaptive clipping: set per-parameter clipping threshold based on importance percentile
steps.step7: Run for CIFAR-10, ResNet-18, epsilon=3 (total, including pretraining), seeds=[42, 123, 456]
steps.step8: Apply post-hoc compression after training and evaluate

experiments.title: PHCA-DP-SGD: Full Method Implementation
experiments.description: Implement the proposed Post-Hoc Compression-Aware DP-SGD with continuous per-parameter clipping weights

steps.step2: Key component 1 - Survival probability estimation: Maintain EMA of per-parameter gradient magnitudes: s_j^(t) = 0.9*s_j^(t-1) + 0.1*|g_j^(t)|
steps.step3: Convert to survival probability: p_j(kappa) = rank_percentile(s_j) where kappa is target sparsity (e.g., 70%)
steps.step4: Key component 2 - Per-parameter clipping weights: w_j = p_j + alpha*(1-p_j) where alpha=0.1 (discount factor for likely-pruned params)
steps.step5: Modify clipping: g_bar_i^(j) = g_i^(j) / max(1, |g_i^(j)| / (C * w_j))
steps.step6: Key component 3 - Compression-aware noise: Sigma_jj = (1 - p_j)^beta where beta=0.5, calibrate base noise to achieve target epsilon
steps.step7: Integrate with Opacus by customizing the DPOptimizer: override clip_and_accumulate and add_noise methods
steps.step8: Test implementation: Verify privacy accounting with RDP (Rényi Differential Privacy) using opacus.accountants.RDPAccountant

experiments.title: PHCA-DP-SGD Training on CIFAR-10
experiments.description: Train PHCA-DP-SGD on CIFAR-10 with ResNet-18 across multiple privacy budgets and compression ratios

steps.step1: Create training script: src/train_phca.py with arguments for epsilon, sparsity, seed
steps.step3: For each configuration: Train PHCA-DP-SGD for 30 epochs with compression-aware objective
steps.step4: During training: Log per-parameter clipping weights statistics (mean, std, min, max across layers)
steps.step5: After training: Apply actual post-hoc magnitude pruning with target sparsity
steps.step6: Evaluate: Test accuracy before compression, test accuracy after compression
steps.step8: Total runs: 3 epsilons × 3 sparsities × 3 seeds = 27 runs for CIFAR-10 ResNet-18

experiments.title: PHCA-DP-SGD on CIFAR-100 and Purchase-100
experiments.description: Validate generalization across datasets with different characteristics

steps.step1: CIFAR-100: ResNet-18, epsilon=[3, 8], sparsity=[0.7], seeds=[42, 123, 456] - 6 runs
steps.step2: Purchase-100: 3-layer MLP (input=600, hidden=[512,256], output=100), epsilon=[3, 8], sparsity=[0.7], seeds=[42, 123, 456] - 6 runs
steps.step3: Adjust hyperparameters for Purchase: epochs=20, batch_size=512, lr=0.05
steps.step4: Run both standard DP-SGD and PHCA-DP-SGD for comparison
steps.step5: Record accuracy before and after compression for all runs

experiments.title: Ablation Study: Component Analysis
experiments.description: Systematically remove each component of PHCA-DP-SGD to measure individual contributions

steps.step1: Ablation 1 - No per-parameter clipping: Set all w_j = 1.0 (uniform clipping), keep compression-aware noise
steps.step2: Ablation 2 - No compression-aware noise: Use isotropic noise, keep per-parameter clipping
steps.step3: Ablation 3 - Fixed survival probabilities: Use initial random survival probabilities instead of estimated from training dynamics
steps.step4: Ablation 4 - Binary masking instead of continuous: Convert w_j to binary (w_j = 1 if p_j > 0.5 else 0.1)
steps.step5: Run all ablations on CIFAR-10, ResNet-18, epsilon=3, sparsity=0.7, seeds=[42, 123, 456]
steps.step6: Compare against full PHCA-DP-SGD to quantify each component's contribution

experiments.title: Hyperparameter Sensitivity Analysis
experiments.description: Analyze sensitivity to key hyperparameters: alpha (discount factor) and beta (noise reduction rate)

steps.step1: Vary alpha: [0.0, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0] where alpha=1.0 recovers standard DP-SGD
steps.step2: Vary beta: [0.0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0] where beta=0.0 means no noise reduction
steps.step4: Run on CIFAR-10, ResNet-18, epsilon=3, sparsity=0.7, seed=42
steps.step5: Create heatmap: test accuracy as function of (alpha, beta)
steps.step6: Identify optimal region and verify robustness around alpha=0.1, beta=0.5

experiments.title: Membership Inference Attack Evaluation
experiments.description: Evaluate empirical privacy using membership inference attacks to validate that compression doesn't increase vulnerability

steps.step1: Implement MIA in src/evaluation/membership_inference.py using loss-based attack
steps.step2: For each trained model (standard DP-SGD, PHCA-DP-SGD, pre-pruning, AdaDPIGU):
steps.step3: Split training set: 45K members for training, 5K members for MIA evaluation
steps.step4: Use 5K test samples as non-members for MIA evaluation
steps.step5: Compute per-sample loss on member and non-member sets
steps.step6: Train attack model (simple threshold on loss) or use threshold-based attack
steps.step7: Metrics: MIA accuracy (lower is better), MIA AUC (lower is better)
steps.step8: Run before and after compression to verify compression doesn't increase leakage

experiments.title: Statistical Significance Testing
experiments.description: Perform rigorous statistical tests to validate performance improvements

steps.step1: Load all results: standard DP-SGD + compression, Adamczewski, AdaDPIGU, PHCA-DP-SGD
steps.step2: For each dataset and configuration, perform paired t-test between methods
steps.step3: Test 1: PHCA-DP-SGD vs Standard DP-SGD + compression (expected: p < 0.05)
steps.step4: Test 2: PHCA-DP-SGD vs Adamczewski pre-pruning (expected: comparable, no significant difference)
steps.step5: Test 3: PHCA-DP-SGD vs AdaDPIGU (expected: p < 0.05 for post-hoc compression scenario)
steps.step6: Report effect sizes (Cohen's d) along with p-values
steps.step7: Create summary table: method comparison with significance indicators

experiments.title: Success Criteria Validation
experiments.description: Evaluate whether success criteria from proposal are met

steps.step1: Criterion 1: Check if PHCA-DP-SGD achieves significantly better accuracy (p<0.05) than standard DP-SGD + compression across datasets
steps.step2: Criterion 2: Check if PHCA-DP-SGD matches Adamczewski performance without using public data
steps.step3: Criterion 3: Check if PHCA-DP-SGD outperforms AdaDPIGU on post-hoc compression tasks
steps.step4: Criterion 4: Validate theoretical claim by checking correlation between survival probability estimates and actual pruned parameters
steps.step5: If criteria not met: Document negative results, analyze potential causes, suggest improvements
steps.step6: Generate pass/fail report for each criterion with supporting evidence

experiments.title: Generate Paper Figures
experiments.description: Create publication-quality figures for the research paper

steps.step1: Figure 1 - Privacy-Utility Trade-off: Plot test accuracy vs epsilon for all methods on CIFAR-10 (epsilons=[1,3,8], sparsity=0.7)
steps.step2: Figure 2 - Compression Robustness: Plot accuracy vs sparsity ratio (0.5, 0.7, 0.9) for epsilon=3
steps.step3: Figure 3 - Method Comparison Bar Chart: Compare PHCA-DP-SGD vs baselines at epsilon=3, sparsity=0.7 with error bars (std across 3 seeds)
steps.step4: Figure 4 - Ablation Study: Bar chart showing accuracy for full method vs ablations
steps.step5: Figure 5 - Hyperparameter Sensitivity: Heatmap of accuracy vs (alpha, beta)
steps.step6: Figure 6 - MIA Comparison: Bar chart of MIA accuracy for different methods
steps.step7: Figure 7 - Survival Probability Correlation: Scatter plot of estimated p_j vs actual |theta_j| after training
steps.step8: Save all figures to results/plots/ as PDF (vector) and PNG (raster)

experiments.title: Generate Paper Tables
experiments.description: Create LaTeX-ready tables for the paper

steps.step1: Table 1 - Main Results: Dataset, Method, Epsilon, Sparsity, Accuracy (mean±std), MIA Accuracy
steps.step2: Table 2 - Ablation Study: Component removed, Accuracy, Relative drop
steps.step3: Table 3 - Cross-Dataset Generalization: Results on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Purchase-100
steps.step4: Table 4 - Comparison with Pre-Pruning: Standard DP-SGD, Adamczewski, PHCA-DP-SGD with/without public data
steps.step5: Save as LaTeX tables in results/tables/ and as CSV for easy viewing

steps.step1: Different architectures: Test ConvNet on CIFAR-10 (epsilon=3, sparsity=0.7, seeds=[42,123,456])
steps.step2: Different compression types: Test quantization (8-bit) instead of pruning
steps.step3: Different target sparsities: Verify method works at extreme sparsity (95%)
steps.step4: Early stopping analysis: Check if method benefits from different stopping criteria
steps.step5: Layer-wise analysis: Examine which layers benefit most from compression-aware training

runtime_estimate.parallelization_notes: Due to Opacus memory requirements and single GPU, experiments run sequentially. However, different epsilon/sparsity configurations are independent and could be parallelized if multiple GPUs available.

expected_key_results.primary: PHCA-DP-SGD achieves 3-5% higher accuracy than standard DP-SGD + compression at epsilon=3, sparsity=0.7 on CIFAR-10
expected_key_results.secondary_1: PHCA-DP-SGD matches Adamczewski pre-pruning without requiring public data
expected_key_results.secondary_2: PHCA-DP-SGD outperforms AdaDPIGU binary masking on post-hoc compression tasks
expected_key_results.tertiary: Ablation study shows both per-parameter clipping and compression-aware noise contribute significantly

fallback_plan.if_exceeds_time_budget: Reduce seeds from 3 to 1 for ablations and hyperparameter sensitivity
fallback_plan.if_memory_issues: Reduce batch size to 128 and accumulate gradients
fallback_plan.if_cifar100_slow: Skip CIFAR-100 and focus on CIFAR-10 + Purchase-100
fallback_plan.if_convergence_issues: Increase epochs to 50 and adjust learning rate schedule
